articles.title: Quick hits: Ecuador does the impossible, Sweden trains the house down

children.content: Ecuador's wildest dreams come true, the Swedes get a rude shock at training and a piece of World Cup history goes up for sale. Here are today's quick hits.

image.altText: Ecuador players and fans during Germany game, celebrating and screaming.

articles.title: Live table: Which third-placed teams will make it to the knockouts?

children.content: The World Cup group stage is winding down, with third-placed teams anxiously watching other results to learn their fate. See the live table to see who is on track to progress.

image.altText: The Socceroos huddle in a circle

children.content: The Socceroos have made a stunning six changes to their starting line-up for the crucial clash with Paraguay. Follow live.

items.title: Socceroos coach stuns with six changes as Paraguay clash begins

updates.title: Who can the Socceroos play in the round of 32?

items.title: Stefanovic's move to outsider politics follows a well-worn lucrative path

children.content: Listening to the culture war showman schtick he has so quickly adopted, Karl Stefanovic is now less a newsman than an ideological performer.

image.altText: Two men in black on a street.

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: Stefanovic thanks audience for 21 years on Today after 'tough' few days

children.content: Today show host Karl Stefanovic will leave Channel Nine after a controversial podcast interview with British far-right figure Tommy Robinson.

image.altText: Karl Stefanovic smiling.

images.altText: A woman walks past a damaged building

articles.title: Thousands feared dead in Venezuela quakes as rescuers search rubble

children.content: Jorge Rodríguez, who heads Venezuela's national assembly, says at least 235 people have died, 1,520 people have been injured, and at least 250 buildings have been damaged or destroyed.

image.altText: Man standing on rubble.

articles.title: Videos show destruction after twin earthquakes hit Venezuela

children.content: ABC NEWS Verify has authenticated videos showing the devastation after twin earthquakes struck Venezuela's coast.

image.altText: A destroyed hotel.
Building collapses

children.content: More than 250 buildings are ruined or damaged after the devastating double quake, president of the National Asembly Jorge Rodríguez says. Follow live.

items.title: Death toll rises to at least 235 as major rescue operation continues

updates.title: 📹: Building collapses after earthquakes in  La Guaira
Venezuelans in Australia anxiously wait to hear from loved ones

items.title: Comparing Trump's Iran negotiations to what Obama already did

children.content: The Trump administration has been negotiating directly with Iran this week in an effort to reach a long-term peace agreement, but it has several hurdles to clear before it can better the deal the United States already had.

image.altText: A composite image of the two leaders in suits

items.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation after ship reports attack

children.content: The UN pauses its operation to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz after a vessel reports an attack.

image.altText: A ship in the forground and another moving a long the horizon the background.

items.title: PM flags strengthening powers of eSafety commissioner for social media ban

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says the government will look to strengthen the powers of the eSafety commissioner to enforce an under-16s social media ban. Follow live.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit with a coat, a red tie and glasses.

items.title: 'An outright lie': Minister challenges Butler on Thriving Kids rollout

children.content: A member of the Thriving Kids advisory panel has voiced concern about how the support program for children with autism and developmental delay is being rolled out.

image.altText: A close up of a kindergartener's hands on a table

items.title: King Charles won't move into Buckingham palace, breaking royal tradition

children.content: An annual financial statement released on Thursday contained several revelations, including plans to cut the royal family's public funding, and details of how much tax the monarch paid.

gallery.altText: A man looking on and smiling, wearing formal dress.
Several ceremonial guards, in front of a large palace.

thumbnail.altText: A man looking on and smiling, wearing formal dress.
Several ceremonial guards, in front of a large palace.

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

items.title: ASX opens higher as ACCC announces anti-price gouging law

children.content: The ASX opens higher in morning trading after mixed results on Wall Street. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: An electronic billboard showing stock prices is seen in a window reflection.

items.title: Man who fatally shot officer Keith Smith sentenced to 40 years in jail

children.content: Tasmanian man Leigh Sushames shot dead Keith Smith last year when the police constable tried to serve him with a home repossession notice.

image.altText: Leigh Sushames looks at the camera.

items.title: H5N1 emerged in Asia 30 years ago. Here's what happened next

children.content: Experts say Asia's decades-long experience fighting bird flu has shown there is no magic bullet and that fragmented responses to the disease will fail.

gallery.altText: A chicken pokes its head out of a red crate while a man in a protective mask and hair net stands behind.

thumbnail.altText: A chicken pokes its head out of a red crate while a man in a protective mask and hair net stands behind.

items.title: Warning fire ants could kill thousands of pets a year

children.content: If Australia does not ramp up fire ant eradication efforts, the invasive species will cause billion-dollar economic impacts and dozens of deaths, a study finds.

image.altText: A fire ant nest

items.title: Daughter arrested after mother's body found at Sydney aged care home

children.content: Police question a 53-year-old woman in Sydney's west about an allegedly administered substance after her mother was found dead in an aged care home.

image.altText: Darrin wears a police hat and uniform and speaks into media microphones.

items.title: Supergirl shoots for the moon, but runs out of fuel

children.content: Australian Milly Alcock shines as Supergirl in the hero's first solo outing since 1984. If only the rest of the movie sparkled around her.

items.title: Friday news quiz: Neil the Seal, the teals party down and Trump drains his swamp

children.content: This week's news quiz features the usual assortment of political animals and regular animals, as well as other odd happenings from across the world.

image.altText: Two people attempt to force a seal off a road.

items.title: Are these the 20 best albums of 2026 so far?

children.content: If you haven't had the time to keep up with new music this year, we've got you covered. Find your new favourite album here.

image.altText: Collage of five performers against a red background, including RAYE with mic and others posed side by side

items.title: A failed forest utopia will watch nervously as the Socceroos play Paraguay

children.content: In rural Paraguay, a small farming town flies Australian flags. When Paraguay plays Australia at the 2026 World Cup today, its residents will be at the edge of their seats.

gallery.altText: A silver hatchback parked beside a small general store on a road covered with red dirt.

thumbnail.altText: A silver hatchback parked beside a small general store on a road covered with red dirt.
A fading sign in Spanish, but featuring the words Nueva Australia

items.title: Man suing Spirit of Tasmania operator told he 'should have sat' on ship's toilet

children.content: A man who claims to have sustained injuries after falling over while using a toilet at sea says the ferry operator was negligent, the federal court has heard.

image.altText: A large white and yellow cruise ship sits in the waterfront of a city.

items.title: Police shoot and critically injure unarmed man charging at officers

children.content: A man has undergone emergency surgery after he was shot by police in Western Sydney.

image.altText: A police van parked on a street surrounded by police tape.

items.title: SA Police officers 'did not act appropriately' in handling of DV survivor's case

children.content: South Australia Police has admitted that three officers did not "act appropriately" in how they handled the case of domestic violence victim Stella Magnisalis, who was assaulted multiple times by her then police detective husband.

image.altText: A woman looks to the right sadly in front of an SA Police logo

items.title: Barring 'ISIS bride' could have triggered High Court challenge

children.content: Legal experts say the Commonwealth would have risked losing a High Court challenge to counterterrorism laws if it stopped the Australian woman from returning home.

image.altText: A group of three women wearing burqas walk along a camp in Syria.

items.title: Mixed reaction to beach town at forefront of over-50s lifestyle resort boom

children.content: Four over-50s lifestyle developments have been approved for this small beach community, with two more being assessed. Some worry the area is not ready for the influx, others say it is the answer to the housing crisis.

gallery.altText: A man with sunglasses and a hat and a woman smiling sitting on a rock at the beach with a golden retriever

thumbnail.altText: A man with sunglasses and a hat and a woman smiling sitting on a rock at the beach with a golden retriever

avatar.alt: A woman in a black shirt with long hair smiling at the camera

items.title: Terminally ill 72-year-old still chasing solo sailing dreams

children.content: David Burston had only just bought an old yacht to fix up and sail around Australia when he was diagnosed with stage 4 cancer. Hee has decided to keep pursuing his dream for as long as he can.

image.altText: Older man wearing sunglasses standing next to boat

items.title: Dezi Freeman case leads to police gun carry changes

children.content: Chief Commissioner Mike Bush says Victoria Police officers in the state's alpine region will soon be permitted to carry long-arm rifles after the Dezi Freeman case.

image.altText: A man with white hair in a navy uniform with badges stares off camera.

items.title: Farmers claim partial victory after government backflips on gas

children.content: Farmers are claiming a partial victory after the Queensland government backed down on controversial changes to gas mining approvals above a vital underground water source.

image.altText: farmers holding placards

items.title: Matthew Guy speaks after Moira Deeming assault allegation

children.content: A Victoria Police spokesperson says a "thorough investigation" into Moira Deeming's claim she was assaulted by fellow Liberal MP Matthew Guy concluded "there was no offence detected".

image.altText: Moira Deeming and Matthew Guy at an event

items.title: 'Not everyone wants to go for a run': Why young adults joined rubbish club

children.content: From recently single, to expats, paid and free clubs designed to help adults make friends are attracting people from all walks of life. As Australians increasingly report feeling lonely, these are the people who are branching out to change that.

gallery.altText: three photographs sit on a wooden table next to a glass cup and a uno card

thumbnail.altText: three photographs sit on a wooden table next to a glass cup and a uno card

items.title: New bridge announced but Great Western Highway closed for another year

children.content: A new bridge will be built at Mitchell's Causeway on the Great Western Highway to link Sydney and the state's central west.

image.altText: A picture of a road with no cars and closure signs

items.title: Industry leaders turn to 3D printing to tackle housing crisis

children.content: With a wealth of job opportunities but no houses for skilled workers, a regional WA town is hoping 3D-printed homes can solve the shortage.

image.altText: A man in a blue shirt and black jacket stands to the left of a woman in a brown dress, both looking at the camera.

items.title: 'Incredibly special moment': Australian underdog makes it to Wimbledon

children.content: Australia's Dane Sweeny makes it through to an overseas grand slam for the first time after a hard-fought victory in the Wimbledon qualifiers.

image.altText: A tennis player pumps his fist and screams in joy after winning a point.

items.title: Veteran fears budget changes 'going to cause more suicides'

children.content: An Australian Defence Force veteran says caps on allied health spending for veterans are a betrayal and that any politician supportive of the changes would not be welcome at Anzac Day events.

image.altText: A man in army uniform.

items.title: Jetskiers rescue 'Princess Peach' the turtle stranded on Magnetic Island

children.content: A group of jetski riders were touring Magnetic Island when they stumbled across an injured turtle.

gallery.altText: A large green sea turtle is strapped to a lifeguard ski

thumbnail.altText: A large green sea turtle is strapped to a lifeguard ski
